raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California has lost 120,000 affordable homes since 2020.",
    "There are over 324,000 homes at risk of losing their affordability by next year in California.",
    "AB 919 would help stabilize communities by preserving and increasing the supply of permanent affordable housing.",
    "Successful local policies like TOPA/COPA in California demonstrate that preserving existing rental housing keeps low-income households in their homes.",
    "Preserving existing rental housing on the private market increases the supply of deed-restricted affordable housing more swiftly and cost-effectively than new construction.",
    "The Stable Homes Act would have mitigated some significant destabilization effects of corporate ownership on communities.",
    "The Stable Homes Act would provide working-class families and BIPOC communities an opportunity to build intergenerational wealth through homeownership.",
    "Protection and preservation of affordable housing are key strategies to prevent homelessness.",
    "AB 919 would have given communities and non-profits the opportunity to keep working families housed and maintain diverse neighborhoods.",
    "Enacting solutions to preserve naturally occurring affordable housing is crucial to addressing California's housing needs."
  ]
}
```
